inline_comments.message:
when I use onap-tests for my end to end tests, I assume that the clouds.yaml will be provided as an input for my testcases. I do not duplicate the opnestack config and rely on shade to play with these parameters.

see https://gitlab.com/Orange-OpenSource/lfn/onap/onap-tests/blob/master/onap_tests/xtesting/freeradius_nbi.py

Is putting below cloud settings into shade library format compatible "clouds.yaml" file what you're suggesting? I.e. put below settings into clouds.yaml in vcpe file tree scope so it can be consumed as input for testcase?

I just wonder if you need to put such configuration here as it should be already available in a clouds.yaml file that should be able to get. So an "official" yaml could be used as a complement to your config.
It would avoid data duplcation (I do not like config duplication.. :)).
But it means that in the rest of the code you need to retrieve the data from this clouds.yaml (e.g. with shade) and from this config
It is up to you.

Ok i see your point. Considering shade to be looking for clouds.yaml first in local dir and only then in ~/.config/openstack I can change the scripts to be using shade library for the OS credentials gathering but will need to elaborate whether search order can be reversed.

Will raise a separate ticket for that matter, let's please accept this one and merge whole topic, will work on top of it.
